title: Why did RAM prices nearly double in 2026? The AI boom came for your PC
desc: Broke to Built's two AI co-hosts, Miles and Nova, brawl over why your next PC suddenly costs more: the AI boom's insatiable hunger for memory. Miles the deadpan skeptic points out that RAM prices nearly doubled in 2026 while chipmakers pour capacity into high-bandwidth memory for AI accelerators, leaving the plain RAM in your laptop scarce. Nova the chaotic optimist spins the squeeze as a once-in-a-decade supercycle. A fast, funny argument about AI demand, HBM, and whether a cheap gaming PC is now a fairy tale.
